title: Sago Pudding – Frog's Eyes

seo.title: Sago Pudding - Frog's Eyes - Marinda Kook

html:
The Cape cold has settled into my bones. It's Sunday and my sweet tooth is nagging at me. So today my decision falls on Frog's Eyes, just as I remember my mother making it.

 

  1. 250 ml sago
  2. 1l milk
  3. 4 eggs separated and 2 more egg whites (cook the yolks tomorrow morning for breakfast)
  4. 30 ml butter
  5. 1 piece of stick cinnamon
  6. 125ml sugar
  7. 5 ml vanilla essence
  8. 5ml salt
  9. 100ml sugar to go with the egg white
  10. apricot jam as much as you like

NOW:

  • if you wake up, let the sago soak in the milk – if you don't do this, it's no problem – you just cook it a little longer.
  • on the stove, pour the milk, the sago and the cinnamon stick into a pot. Cook it until the sago turns translucent – watch out for it burning. (keep the heat low). Now stir in the butter.
  • in another bowl whisk the egg yolks, the 125ml sugar and the vanilla essence together. Once the sago is translucent, stir it into the milk mixture on the stove.
  • spoon the sago mixture into an ovenproof dish and dot it with apricot jam
  • whisk the egg whites in a separate bowl until almost stiff. Now add the 100ml sugar bit by bit
  • spoon this on top of the sago mixture in the ovenproof dish
  • bake it for 20 minutes at 160 degrees Celsius and then for another 15 minutes at 190 degrees Celsius

This is truly soul food that will chase away even the coldest winter.
